# nestjs-langchain-tools

> Opinionated NestJS × LangChain integration: one decorator per agent (`@ToolsAgent`), one per tool (`@AgentTool`), auto-discovered at boot, streaming by default, observability auto-wired (LangSmith + token/cost), and shipped adapters for Redis / Mongo / Qdrant / pgvector / Pinecone. LangGraph-first — the default coordinator is `GraphCoordinatorService` built on `createReactAgent` (flat) or a `StateGraph` supervisor (multi-agent). Tool inputs are **class-validator DTOs** (no Zod required), converted to JSON Schema for the LLM and validated at runtime.

This file is an LLM-friendly implementation guide for the package. Follow it to produce correct, idiomatic apps on the first try.

## Package identity

- npm: `nestjs-langchain-tools` (current line: v0.3.x)
- NestJS: `^11` (peer) · `reflect-metadata` is required
- LangChain: `^1.x` (`@langchain/core`, `@langchain/openai`, `@langchain/langgraph` …)
- Runtime: Node 18+; `tsconfig` target must support async generators (`es2021`+) if you use streaming tools
- License: MIT

## When to use this package

Use it when you are building a NestJS backend that needs LLM agents and tools. It replaces hand-rolled `OpenAIService` wrappers, custom decorator-on-`AgentExecutor` layers, and the "LangChain imports leaking across controllers" pattern.

Do NOT use it when:
- You are outside NestJS (no DI, no `@nestjs/core`). The package is tightly integrated with `DiscoveryService`.
- You need a bare chat completion with no tools. Call the LangChain model directly — this package targets tool-using agents.
- You need LangChain features the package does not wrap (specific chains, non-agent workflows). Use LangChain directly for those; import the raw model from `@langchain/openai` etc.

## Golden path (copy this shape)

```ts
// app.module.ts
import { Module } from '@nestjs/common';
import { LangChainToolsModule, ModelProvider } from 'nestjs-langchain-tools';
import { WeatherAgent } from './weather.agent';
import { ChatController } from './chat.controller';

@Module({
  imports: [
    LangChainToolsModule.forRootAsync({
      useFactory: () => ({
        coordinatorProvider: ModelProvider.OPENAI,
        coordinatorModel: 'gpt-4o',
        coordinatorUseMemory: true,
      }),
    }),
  ],
  controllers: [ChatController],
  providers: [WeatherAgent],
})
export class AppModule {}
```

```ts
// weather.agent.ts
import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
import { IsString, IsOptional, IsEnum } from 'class-validator';
import { ToolsAgent, AgentTool } from 'nestjs-langchain-tools';

class GetWeatherDto {
  @IsString() location!: string;
  @IsEnum(['celsius', 'fahrenheit']) @IsOptional() unit?: 'celsius' | 'fahrenheit';
}

@Injectable()
@ToolsAgent({
  name: 'WeatherAgent',
  description: 'Answers weather questions',
  systemPrompt: 'You are a weather expert. Use the tool for every question.',
})
export class WeatherAgent {
  @AgentTool({
    name: 'get_weather',
    description: 'Current weather at a location',
    input: GetWeatherDto,
  })
  async getWeather(input: GetWeatherDto): Promise<string> {
    return `It's 22°${input.unit === 'fahrenheit' ? 'F' : 'C'} in ${input.location}.`;
  }
}
```

```ts
// chat.controller.ts
import { Controller } from '@nestjs/common';
import { LangChainChatController } from 'nestjs-langchain-tools/http';
import { GraphCoordinatorService } from 'nestjs-langchain-tools';

@Controller('chat')
export class ChatController extends LangChainChatController {
  constructor(protected coordinator: GraphCoordinatorService) { super(); }
}
```

That yields: `POST /chat` (sync reply), `GET /chat/stream?q=...&sessionId=...` (SSE), `GET /chat/healthz`.

## Hard rules (do not violate)

- **One decorator per layer.** Exactly `@Injectable()` + `@ToolsAgent()` on the class; `@AgentTool()` on methods. Do not hand-instantiate `AgentExecutor` / `createReactAgent` — the module does it from discovered metadata.
- **Tool inputs are DTOs, not Zod.** Prefer `input: MyDto` (class-validator). Use `schema: z.object(...)` only when interop with an existing Zod schema is unavoidable. Never mix both on the same tool.
- **DTO fields must carry class-validator decorators** (`@IsString`, `@IsInt`, `@IsEnum`, `@IsOptional`, etc.). Fields without a validator become `unknown` in JSON Schema and the LLM cannot target them.
- **Agents are Nest providers.** List every agent class in a module's `providers: []`. Without this, `DiscoveryService` cannot find them and the coordinator sees zero tools.
- **Never call `processMessage*` before bootstrap.** Services resolve agents in `OnApplicationBootstrap`. Inject `GraphCoordinatorService` — don't construct it yourself.
- **`sessionId` is the memory primary key.** Always pass it on concurrent / multi-user traffic. `"default"` is a last-resort fallback, not a production value.
- **Streaming tools must be `async function*`** and declared with `streaming: true`. Don't return a Promise from a generator tool — return via the generator's `return` statement (the value becomes the tool-end payload).
- **`@SupervisorAgent` workers must match `@ToolsAgent({ name })` exactly** (case-sensitive). A typo silently drops the worker.
- **Module options are validated by class-validator at boot.** A misconfiguration throws `ModuleOptionsValidationError` listing every failing constraint. Read the message — don't silence the error.

## Core API at a glance

Decorators (`nestjs-langchain-tools`):
- `@ToolsAgent({ name, description, systemPrompt, modelType?, modelName?, temperature?, streaming?, useMemory?, retrieval? })` — class decorator.
- `@AgentTool({ name, description, input?, schema?, streaming?, timeout? })` — method decorator.
- `@SupervisorAgent({ name, workers, routingStrategy?, systemPrompt?, taskDelegation?, terminateOn?, finalSynthesis? })` — class decorator; multi-agent orchestration.
- `@WithRetrieval({ enabled, collectionName, topK?, scoreThreshold?, includeMetadata?, storeRetrievedContext? })` — RAG per-agent.
- `@Authorize({ roles?, policy?, attributes? })` — deny-by-default via a configured `toolAuthorizer`.
- `@HumanInterrupt({ prompt?, reason? })` — pause execution pending `coordinator.resume(threadId, decision)`.
- `@SessionId()` — param decorator; resolves `x-session-id` → `req.user.id` → `req.user.sub` → `"default"`.

Services (inject where needed):
- `GraphCoordinatorService` — **default**. `processMessage(q, { sessionId })` / `processMessageStream(q, { sessionId })` / `resume(threadId, input)`.
- `CoordinatorService` — legacy v0.2 coordinator; still registered for migration. Prefer the graph one.
- `TokenUsageService` — `.totals(sessionId?)`, `.breakdown()`, `.setPricing()`. Auto-wired — no config needed.
- `VectorStoreService` — `.addDocuments(docs, collection, opts)`, `.similaritySearch(...)`.
- `ToolStreamService` — manual progress API; only needed if NOT using async-generator tools.
- `MemoryService` — low-level session store access.
- `LangChainHealthIndicator` / `LangChainTerminusIndicator` (`/health`) — readiness probes.

Module: `LangChainToolsModule.forRoot(opts)` / `.forRootAsync({ imports, inject, useFactory })`.

Key `LangChainToolsModuleOptions` fields: `coordinatorProvider`, `coordinatorModel`, `coordinatorTemperature` (0..2), `coordinatorPrompt`, `coordinatorUseMemory`, `orchestration` (`'auto' | 'flat' | 'supervisor'`), `supervisorTaskDelegation`, `enableStreaming`, `enableToolStreaming`, `toolTimeout`, `sessionStore`, `toolAuthorizer`, `maxMessagesPerSession`, `sessionTtlMs`, `vectorStore`, `embeddingModel`, `llmFactory`, `coordinatorLlm`, `scope`.

## Streaming events (typed)

`processMessageStream()` returns `Observable<CoordinatorStreamEvent>` with `event.type ∈`:
- `token` — streamed token `{ content, agent }`
- `tool-start` — `{ tool, input }`
- `tool-progress` — from async-generator `yield` or `ToolStreamService`
- `tool-end` — `{ tool, output }`
- `agent-handoff` — supervisor routing `{ from, to }`
- `interrupt` — `@HumanInterrupt` fired `{ threadId, reason }`
- `complete` — final `{ content, threadId }`
- `error` — `{ error }`

Always include a `default:` branch in your `switch` — new event types may be added in minor versions.

## Subpath exports

Use these imports (declared in `package.json#exports`, do not deep-import `dist/...`):

- `nestjs-langchain-tools` — core (decorators, module, services, graph).
- `nestjs-langchain-tools/http` — `LangChainChatController`, `ChatRequestBody`, `ServerSentEvent`.
- `nestjs-langchain-tools/testing` — `LangChainToolsTestingModule`, `MockChatModel`.
- `nestjs-langchain-tools/health` — `LangChainHealthIndicator`, `LangChainTerminusIndicator`.
- `nestjs-langchain-tools/graph` — graph internals / `buildSupervisor` / `mapStreamEvent`.
- `nestjs-langchain-tools/redis` — `RedisSessionStore`, `createRedisCheckpointSaver` (peer: `ioredis` or `redis`).
- `nestjs-langchain-tools/mongo` — `MongoSessionStore`, `createMongoCheckpointSaver`, `MongoAtlasVectorStore` (peer: `mongodb`).
- `nestjs-langchain-tools/qdrant` — `QdrantVectorStore` (peer: `@qdrant/js-client-rest`).
- `nestjs-langchain-tools/pgvector` — `PgVectorStore` (peer: `pg`, Postgres pgvector extension).
- `nestjs-langchain-tools/pinecone` — `PineconeVectorStore` (peer: `@pinecone-database/pinecone`).

All adapters take a user-supplied client. **The library never opens or closes connections.**

## Common pitfalls

- **Zero tools discovered.** The agent class is missing from `providers: []`, OR the module is not imported in the root module. Fix the wiring; discovery only scans registered providers.
- **DTO field ignored by the LLM.** A field without class-validator decorators is omitted from the generated JSON Schema. Add at least one validator, even `@IsOptional()` + a type check.
- **`ModuleOptionsValidationError` at boot.** A primitive option failed constraint validation (e.g. `coordinatorTemperature: 3` breaks `@Max(2)`). The error lists every issue — fix them verbatim.
- **Stream never completes.** The caller unsubscribed early, or an uncaught error inside a tool. Always add an `error:` handler on the Observable subscription. SSE clients must also close the `EventSource` on `complete`.
- **Concurrent users trample each other's memory.** No `sessionId` passed → everything falls into the `"default"` bucket. Use `@SessionId()` in the controller.
- **Human-in-the-loop hangs.** `resume(threadId, ...)` was never called. The `threadId` arrives in the `interrupt` event — persist it and bind the resume call to the operator's approval UI.
- **Supervisor silently ignores a worker.** `workers: ['WheatherAgent']` with a typo doesn't match any `@ToolsAgent({ name })`. Worker names are string-matched exact.
- **LangGraph missing.** `GraphCoordinatorService` / `@SupervisorAgent` / `@HumanInterrupt` require `@langchain/langgraph` installed. The legacy `CoordinatorService` works without it.
- **Mixed Zod + class-validator on one tool.** Pick one. `input` wins over `schema` if both are set, but ambiguous configs are an anti-pattern.
- **Importing from `dist/`.** Use the public subpath exports above — deep imports are not part of the public API.

## Observability & cost

- **LangSmith tracing is automatic** when `LANGSMITH_API_KEY` (or `LANGCHAIN_API_KEY`) is set. No code change needed.
- **Token + cost tracking is always on**. Inject `TokenUsageService`. `.totals(sessionId?)` returns `{ promptTokens, completionTokens, totalTokens, costUsd }`. Override pricing via `.setPricing({ modelId: { input, output } })`.
- **Retries**: `withRetry(fn, { maxAttempts, initialDelayMs, maxDelayMs, factor })` — exponential backoff with jitter, retries 429 / 5xx / network errors by default.
- **Timeouts**: `toolTimeout: { enabled: true, durationMs: 30_000 }` globally, or `timeout` on a specific `@AgentTool`.

## Testing

```ts
import { Test } from '@nestjs/testing';
import { LangChainToolsTestingModule, MockChatModel } from 'nestjs-langchain-tools/testing';

const mock = new MockChatModel({
  script: [
    { toolCalls: [{ name: 'get_weather', args: { location: 'Paris' } }] },
    'The weather in Paris is sunny.',
  ],
});
const mod = await Test.createTestingModule({
  imports: [LangChainToolsTestingModule.forRoot({ llm: mock }), MyAgentsModule],
}).compile();
```

Prefer `MockChatModel` over mocking the HTTP layer — it drives the same `BaseChatModel` contract the coordinator expects.

## Escape hatches

- `llmFactory: (ctx) => BaseChatModel` — swap the default provider map entirely. Receives agent context (name, provider hint, etc.).
- `coordinatorLlm: <pre-built model>` — bypass the factory for the coordinator specifically.
- `orchestration: 'flat' | 'supervisor' | 'auto'` — force a mode when auto-detection guesses wrong.
- Adapter interfaces (`SessionStore`, `ToolAuthorizer`, `VectorStore`, `EmbeddingsLike`) are public — implement your own backend when the shipped ones don't fit.

## Non-goals

- Not a LangChain re-exporter. Import `@langchain/openai`, `@langchain/core/messages` etc. directly when needed.
- No built-in auth. `@Authorize` needs a `ToolAuthorizer` that you provide; the library does not know about your JWT / session.
- No connection pooling. Adapters accept a client you own.
- No LangChain chains beyond tool-calling agents and retrieval. Compose chains yourself if needed.

## Reference

- [README.md](./README.md): full prose reference with every feature example
- [MIGRATION-v0.2-to-v0.3.md](./MIGRATION-v0.2-to-v0.3.md): breaking changes, coordinator swap, new event types
- [docs/RAG.md](./docs/RAG.md): retrieval augmentation deep dive — vector store config, chunking, `@WithRetrieval`, `VectorStoreService`
- [docs/STREAMING_TOOLS.md](./docs/STREAMING_TOOLS.md): tool streaming protocol, SSE / fetch consumption, `ToolStreamUpdate` shape
- [src/index.ts](./src/index.ts): authoritative export surface (decorators, services, types, utils)
- [src/config/module-options.ts](./src/config/module-options.ts): full `LangChainToolsModuleOptions` schema with class-validator constraints
- [src/interfaces/agent.interface.ts](./src/interfaces/agent.interface.ts): `AgentOptions` union (`ModelProvider`, per-provider option shapes)
- [src/interfaces/tool.interface.ts](./src/interfaces/tool.interface.ts): `ToolOptions`, `ToolStreamUpdate`, `ToolStreamUpdateType`
- [src/graph/stream-events.ts](./src/graph/stream-events.ts): `CoordinatorStreamEvent` discriminated union — read this before writing a frontend consumer
- [src/decorators/](./src/decorators/): source of truth for every decorator shipped

## Optional

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/arthurmtro/nestjs-langchain-tools): issues, releases, examples under `test/example-app`
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/nestjs-langchain-tools): install and version history
